executive_summary: We maintain thought leadership across sanctions enforcement, tax evasion detection, and illicit finance attribution, with notable wins on Iran IRGC tracking and €1M Ordinals tax fraud. Competitors intensify AI compliance positioning while regulatory clarity on crypto legislation and stablecoin infrastructure accelerates institutional adoption momentum.

narratives.title: Tax Evasion & Novel Asset Class Surveillance
narratives.description: Crypto tax evaders testing Ordinals and inscriptions but leaving traceable blockchain trails; our reports driving enforcement actions across jurisdictions.
narratives.relevance: Our Ordinals tracking and tax fraud attribution continue proving novel asset classes can't escape on-chain scrutiny, positioning us as the go-to intelligence layer for tax authorities.

narratives.title: Sanctions Enforcement & Geopolitical Illicit Attribution
narratives.description: Iran IRGC cryptocurrency infrastructure, Sinaloa Cartel fentanyl trafficking networks, and OFAC designation enforcement remain core illicit finance narratives.
narratives.relevance: Our geopolitical attribution work on Iran and sanctioned entities positions us as indispensable to US Treasury and law enforcement, but faces rising competitive pressure from Arkham and Crystal.

narratives.title: AI & Compliance Automation Infrastructure Arms Race
narratives.description: Elliptic fundraising ($120M) and CEO warnings about AI overwhelming compliance teams signal intensifying competitive positioning around AI-driven financial crime detection.
narratives.relevance: We must accelerate AI automation capabilities and security posture; Elliptic's messaging on AI-trading surge and compliance overwhelm challenges our market positioning if we don't visibly lead on AI integration.

narratives.relevance: Stablecoin growth creates new compliance and surveillance use cases; we should emphasize stablecoin flow tracking and cross-border payment transparency capabilities.

market_landscape.title: Regulatory Tailwinds Accelerating
market_landscape.body: US Senate crypto bill advances, UK-EU frameworks crystallize, and regulatory clarity on prediction markets and market surveillance institutionalizes. This creates sustained demand for compliant blockchain intelligence infrastructure across jurisdictions.

market_landscape.title: Compliance Automation Competitive Intensity
market_landscape.body: Elliptic's $120M raise and AI-driven compliance messaging intensifies competitive positioning; market shifting from forensics-only to integrated AI automation threat. We must visibly lead on predictive compliance and AI security to maintain leadership positioning.

market_landscape.title: Geopolitical Illicit Finance Persistence
market_landscape.body: Iran IRGC, North Korean hacking, and Sinaloa Cartel crypto laundering remain dominant enforcement narratives. Sanctions attribution and illicit network mapping remain core government demand drivers, but competitive entry from TRM and emerging players pressures market share.

competitor_reads.Elliptic: Aggressive AI/ML positioning via CEO thought leadership on compliance overwhelm and security arms race; $120M Series C signals they are scaling threat narrative around AI trading surge to capture compliance automation budgets.
competitor_reads.TRM Labs: Maintaining illicit finance detection and institutional sanctions enforcement positioning; T3 Financial Crime Unit partnership (Tether, TRON) and $450M frozen assets narrative build credibility in sanctions/AML enforcement space competing directly with us.
competitor_reads.Arkham Intelligence: Pivoting to decentralized exchange model while building on-chain intelligence competitive positioning; $4B LAB token narrative and high-profile entity tracking (Grayscale, Bhutan BTC) attempt to capture retail/trader intelligence demand segment.
competitor_reads.Crystal Intelligence: Emerging competitor with focused North Korea attribution work ($7.1B South Korea illicit trace); narrow geopolitical niche positioning but signals expanding competitive threat to our sanctions enforcement dominance.
